data.title: Schedule for US economic data delayed by government shutdown
data.description:
The statistical agencies responsible for publishing data about the U.S. economy have begun updating release schedules following the reporting lapse that occurred during the federal government shutdown.

Following are the latest release dates for data that was missed during the shutdown and any schedule changes for future data related to that.

Release date

Time (U.S. EST)

Report

Period covered

Agency

November 25

8:30

Retail sales

September

Census Bureau

November 25

8:30

Producer Price Index

September

Bureau of Labor Statistics

November 25

10:00

Business inventories

August

Census Bureau

November 26

8:30

Durable goods orders

September

Census Bureau

December 3

8:30

Import and Export prices

September

Bureau of Labor Statistics

December 3

9:15

Industrial production

September

Federal Reserve

December 9

10:00

Job Openings & Labor Turnover Survey*

October

Bureau of Labor Statistics

December 10

8:30

Employment Cost Index

Q3

Bureau of Labor Statistics

December 16

8:30

Employment Situation**

November

Bureau of Labor Statistics

December 18

8:30

Consumer Price Index***

November

Bureau of Labor Statistics

December 18

8:30

Real Earnings****

November

Bureau of Labor Statistics

* - BLS will not publish a September JOLTS report. Data from the survey for September 2025 will be published with the October 2025 data.

** - BLS will not release an Employment Situation report for October, but establishment survey data covering October will be released with the November data. Household survey data from the Current Population Survey were not collected for the October 2025 reference period due to a lapse in appropriations and will not be collected retroactively. For both surveys, the collection period for November 2025 data will be extended, and extra processing time will be needed.

*** - BLS will not release a CPI report for October. BLS is unable to retroactively collect these data. For a few indexes, BLS uses nonsurvey data sources instead of survey data to make the index calculations. BLS is able to retroactively acquire most of the nonsurvey data for October. Where possible, BLS will publish October 2025 values for these series with the release of November 2025 data. The November news release and database update will not include 1-month percent changes for November 2025 where the October 2025 data are missing.

**** - BLS will not publish October 2025 Real Earnings data. BLS could not collect the relevant CPI data for the October reference period. BLS is unable to retroactively collect these data.

data.title: Recover Investment Losses: Class Action Initiated Against Stride, Inc. (LRN)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 21, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Stride, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/stride-inc-lawsuit-submission-form-3?prid=178696&wire=1&utm_campaign=24

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Stride, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 22, 2024 and October 28, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants made false statements and/or concealed that Stride was (1) inflating enrollment numbers by retaining "ghost students"; (2) cutting staffing costs by assigning teachers' caseloads far beyond the required statutory limits; (3) ignoring compliance requirements, including background checks and licensure laws for its employees, and ignoring federally mandated special education services to students; (4) suppressing whistleblowers who documented financial directives from Stride's leadership to delay hiring and deny services to preserve profit margins; and (5) losing existing and potential enrollments.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Stride stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/stride-inc-lawsuit-submission-form-3?prid=178696&wire=1&utm_campaign=24 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

data.title: GMEXICO/B: Revenue and EBITDA grew on mining strength, offsetting declines in other segments
data.description:
Revenues rose 5.7% to $13.02 billion in the first nine months of 2025, led by mining and higher commodity prices, while transportation and infrastructure saw declines. EBITDA grew 9.4% to $7.08 billion, and a strong balance sheet supports ongoing major capital projects.

data.title: VALVOLINE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Valvoline Inc., a leader in automotive preventive maintenance, has released its annual 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of Valvoline's activities, including its focus on expanding its retail network and enhancing shareholder value.

Financial Highlights

Valvoline Inc. reported strong financial performance for the fiscal year, with key metrics showing growth and stability:

  • Net revenues: $1,710.3 million, reflecting a 5.6% increase over the prior year primarily due to higher volume, mix, and pricing.
  • Gross profit: $658.5 million, improved by 6.4% year-over-year driven by higher volume and a favorable mix from premiumization and non-oil change services.
  • Operating income: $389.9 million, increased by 6.2% from the previous year, supported by strong top-line performance and operational efficiencies.
  • Net income: $210.7 million, slightly decreased from $211.5 million in the prior year, impacted by increased income tax expense.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.64, reflecting a slight increase from $1.61 in the previous year, supported by share repurchase activities.

Business Highlights

Valvoline Inc. continues to strengthen its position in the automotive preventive maintenance industry with significant operational achievements:

  • Company Overview: Valvoline operates approximately 2,200 service center locations in the U.S. and Canada through its Valvoline Instant Oil Change and Valvoline Great Canadian Oil Change retail locations, and supports over 240 locations through its Express Care platform.
  • Historical Background: Established in 1866, Valvoline has a long history of innovation in automotive maintenance, including the development of the world's first petroleum-based lubricant.
  • Discontinued Operations: On March 1, 2023, Valvoline completed the sale of its former Global Products segment to Aramco Overseas Company B.V., focusing its operations on retail services.
  • Retail Services: Valvoline offers a range of services including 15-minute oil changes, battery, bulb, and wiper replacements, tire rotations, and other manufacturer-recommended maintenance services, leveraging its proprietary SuperPro system for consistent service delivery.
  • Industry Overview: The automotive aftermarket services market is growing due to an increasing number of vehicles in operation, vehicle complexity, and miles driven, presenting opportunities for consolidation in a fragmented market.
  • Business and Growth Strategies: Valvoline aims to drive shareholder value by maximizing its core business potential, expanding its store network, and innovating to meet evolving customer needs, with a focus on fleet business and non-oil change services.
  • Retail Store Development: Valvoline achieved its 19th consecutive year of same-store sales growth in fiscal 2025, with a network of 2,180 service center stores across the U.S. and Canada, reflecting a nearly 50% growth over the last five years.
  • Competition: Valvoline competes with automotive dealerships, repair centers, and other quick lube operators, focusing on brand recognition, service quality, and customer experience.
  • Marketing and Customer Experience: Valvoline emphasizes a quick, easy, and trusted in-store experience, utilizing digital tools for customer feedback and engagement, and employing targeted marketing strategies to attract and retain customers.
  • Human Capital Management: Valvoline focuses on creating a safe, diverse, and inclusive work environment, offering competitive wages, benefits, and career development opportunities to attract and retain talent.
  • Corporate Citizenship: Valvoline is committed to community service, supporting causes related to children's health and providing financial assistance to employees in need through its Happy to Help initiative.
  • Future Outlook: Valvoline plans to continue expanding its retail network and enhancing its service offerings to meet the changing needs of customers and the automotive market.

Strategic Initiatives

Valvoline has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its business operations and market presence:

  • Store Sales and Acquisitions: The company completed the sale of 67 company-owned stores to franchise partners, which is expected to accelerate growth and deliver long-term shareholder value. Additionally, Valvoline signed an agreement to acquire Breeze Autocare, expanding its footprint with 207 new stores, subject to divestiture of 45 locations to comply with FTC requirements.
  • Capital Management: Valvoline's capital management activities included the issuance of a $740 million Term Loan B to fund the Breeze Autocare acquisition, with excess proceeds allocated to debt reduction. The company repurchased 1.6 million shares for $59.8 million under a $400 million share repurchase authorization, with $325 million remaining available. Valvoline also completed a debt tender offer, repurchasing $598.3 million of its 2030 Notes, reducing interest expenses.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Valvoline plans to continue its strategic focus on expanding its service center network and enhancing shareholder value through acquisitions and refranchising. The company forecasts capital expenditures of $250 million to $280 million for fiscal 2026, primarily funded by operating cash flows, to support growth initiatives. Valvoline aims to maintain a target adjusted EBITDA net leverage ratio of 2.5 to 3.5 times, with a focus on debt reduction following the Breeze Autocare acquisition.

Challenges and Risks

Valvoline faces several significant risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Market Risks: The company operates in a highly competitive market, facing pressure on prices and margins from larger competitors with greater financial resources. Additionally, the adoption of electric vehicles poses a risk to traditional lubricant services, although it also presents opportunities for new service offerings.
  • Operational Risks: Rising and volatile supply costs, along with supply chain constraints, could adversely affect Valvoline's operations. The company is also exposed to risks related to the execution of its growth strategy, including challenges in acquiring and integrating new businesses, and the potential failure of franchisees to meet growth objectives.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes in laws and regulations, particularly those related to environmental, data privacy, and franchising, could increase operational costs and restrict business operations. Compliance with evolving data protection laws is resource-intensive and could lead to significant liabilities if not managed properly.
  • Emerging Risks: The company's recent ERP system implementation has highlighted vulnerabilities in internal controls, which could continue to impact business operations if not effectively managed. Additionally, the sale of Global Products has resulted in a less diversified company, increasing vulnerability to market and economic changes.

Management has identified several strategic initiatives to address these challenges, including enhancing operational efficiency, expanding the retail network, innovating to meet changing customer needs, and addressing supply chain challenges.

SEC Filing: VALVOLINE INC [ VVV ] - 10-K - Nov. 21, 2025

data.title: Brazil's Neoenergia sells hydro power plant Dardanelos to EDF for $463 million
data.description:
Brazilian power company Neoenergia said on Friday it had signed a contract to sell hydro-power plant Dardanelos to EDF Brasil Hidro Participacoes for 2.5 billion reais ($462.72 million).

  • Under the deal, Neoenergia, controlled by Spain's Iberdrola , is selling EAPSA, the firm which runs Dardanelos.

  • The power plant, located in Brazil's centeral-western state of Mato Grosso, has an installed capacity of 261 megawatts.

  • Neoenergia will retain a minority stake in Dardanelos as it also agreed to buy 25% of Hidro Participacoes for 93.5 million reais.

  • EDF Brasil Holding will hold the remaining 75% of Hidro Participacoes after a 280.5 million reais capital injection.

  • EDF Brasil Holding will have the right to buy Neoenergia's stake in Hidro Participacoes within 2.5 years after the deal closes.

  • Dardanelos is the only investment of Hidro Participacoes.

($1 = 5.4028 reais)

data.title: Moolec Receives Nasdaq Determination Letter
data.description:
GEORGE TOWN, CAYMAN ISLANDS / ACCESS Newswire / November 21, 2025 / On November 14, 2025, Moolec Science SA ("Moolec" or the "Company") received a determination letter (the "Nasdaq Determination Letter") from the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC ("Nasdaq") indicating that the closing bid price of the Company's ordinary shares had been below the minimum $1.00 per share requirement for the last 30 consecutive business days, as set forth in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2), and informing the Company of their view that the Company would not be afforded an automatic 180-day period to regain compliance with such rule.

The Company has submitted a timely appeal to a Nasdaq Hearings Panel, through which it will request the granting of a 180- day period to submit and effect a compliance plan, which will include a potential implementation of a reverse stock split, if determined to be in the best interest of shareholders.

The submission of the appeal automatically stays the effect of the Nasdaq Determination Letter until the appeal has been considered, which will take place on December 18, 2025. Accordingly, the Company's ordinary shares will continue to trade on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "MLEC" without interruption during such stay.

The Company intends to continue closely monitoring the closing bid price for its ordinary shares and remains committed to evaluating and executing all appropriate actions to cure the deficiency and regain compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). The receipt of the Nasdaq Determination Letter does not affect the Company's business, operations or reporting requirements with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

About Moolec Science SA

Moolec Science is an innovation-driven company engineering plants and microbes to unlock scalable protein solutions for the global food system. By integrating Molecular Farming and precision fermentation, Moolec combines the cost efficiency of crops with the functionality and nutrition of animal proteins, creating sustainable and commercially viable ingredients. The Company's diversified pipeline includes alternative proteins, bioactive compounds, nutritional oils, and biological inputs for regenerative agriculture. Moolec has a robust intellectual property portfolio with more than 118 granted and pending patents across multiple technology platforms. With operations spanning the United States, Europe and South America, Moolec is advancing a seed-to-ingredient approach designed to transform the future of food production. Moolec is publicly listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "MLEC." For more information, please visit www.moolecscience.com and ir.moolecscience.com.

Forward-looking Statements

This press release contains "forward-looking statements." Forward-looking statements may be identified by the use of words such as "forecast," "intend," "seek," "target," "anticipate," "believe," "expect," "estimate," "plan," "outlook," and "project" and other similar expressions that predict or indicate future events or trends or that are not statements of historical matters. Such forward-looking statements with respect to the Company's plan to regain compliance under the NASDAQ rules, performance, prospects, revenues, and other aspects of the business of Moolec are predictions, projections and other statements about future events that are based on current expectations and assumptions and, as a result, are subject to risks and uncertainties. Although we believe that we have a reasonable basis for each forward-looking statement contained in this press release, we caution you that these statements are based on a combination of facts and factors, about which we cannot be certain. We cannot assure you that the forward-looking statements in this press release will prove accurate. These forward-looking statements are subject to a number of significant risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expected results, including, among others, changes in applicable laws or regulations, the possibility that Moolec may be adversely affected by economic, business and/or other competitive factors, costs related to the scaling up of Moolec's business and other risks and uncertainties, including those included under the header "Risk Factors" in Moolec's Annual Report on Form 20-F filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), as well as Moolec's other filings with the SEC. Should one or more of these risks or uncertainties materialize, or should any of our assumptions prove incorrect, actual results may vary in material respects from those projected in these forward-looking statements. We undertake no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as may be required under applicable securities laws. Accordingly, you should not put undue reliance on these statements.

data.title: US CFPB settles with MoneyLion as enforcement activity winds down
data.description:
Key points:
  • MoneyLion accused of overcharging military personnel on loans
  • Settlement includes $1.75 million payout
  • MoneyLion' disagreed with allegations, pleased to settle

By Jonathan Stempel

The U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau reached a $1.75 million settlement with MoneyLion to settle charges that the online lender overcharged military personnel on loans, in possibly one of the agency's final actions as it winds down enforcement activity.

MoneyLion, a unit of Gen Digital , had been accused in a September 2022 lawsuit filed during the Biden administration of imposing more than the legally permitted 36% on loans to active service members and their families.

The CFPB said the overcharges combined interest rates and membership fees for a program to access low-cost loans, and MoneyLion did not let borrowers cancel memberships until their loans were paid off.

According to a Friday filing in Manhattan federal court, the payout will be distributed, mostly pro rata, to borrowers between December 1, 2017 and October 11, 2024.

MoneyLion also agreed to let borrowers cancel memberships even if they had unpaid loan balances or membership fees.

It did not admit or deny wrongdoing in agreeing to the settlement, which requires court approval.

"While we disagree with the CFPB’s allegations, we are glad to put this matter behind us and focus on continuing to help Americans improve their financial lives," MoneyLion said in a statement.

The Trump administration has been trying to shut down the CFPB, which during Democratic presidencies was an aggressive watchdog over alleged financial misconduct.

Though a federal judge blocked a dismantling in March, acting director Russell Vought has halted most of the CFPB's work as the White House starved the agency of cash needed to operate.

The CFPB is seeking to move its remaining enforcement cases to the U.S. Department of Justice, four people with knowledge of the matter said this week.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--MISO requests FERC's approval for joint planning agreement with electric co-op
data.description: (Platts)--21Nov2025/539 pm EST/2239 GMT **Plan would create a formalized coordination process **Missouri-based AECI is one of MISO's 13 seams partners The Midcontinent Independent System Operator is seeking federal regulatory approval for its first joint planning agreement with seams partner Associated Electric Cooperative Inc. MISO outlined the terms of its proposed agreement with AECI in a Federal Energy Regulatory Commission filing Nov. 21 (ER26-562). The regional transmission organization said the proposed JPA would align AECI's affected systems study process with their respective interconnection queue processes and is intended to be a "foundational document with sufficient guardrails" to implement their current processes. Springfield, Missouri-based AECI is an electric cooperative that provides power for more than 2.1 million member-consumers across rural Missouri, northeast Oklahoma, and southeast Iowa, according to its website. AECI is one of 13 seams partners with which MISO coordinates affected system studies, although it is not subject to FERC jurisdiction. The two also have an emergency energy agreement in place. MISO, which manages the electric grid across 15 states in the US Midwest and South, and AECI already coordinate studies impacting their transmission systems and provide each other with regular status updates on their respective interconnection clusters, according to the filing. Both use this information to determine which projects to study and when to schedule the studies. The proposed JPA would ensure that each entity's cluster study timing is respected, MISO said. It would establish queue priority for interconnection request clusters within each seam partner's transmission system, study processes, and various administrative processes, including a transition process for clusters in progress, according to the filing. MISO is requesting that FERC accept the proposal with an effective date of Jan. 21, 2026. "The seams partners agree that formal study timelines, study financing procedures, and agreement execution deadlines would provide greater certainty to interconnection customers and ensure efficient administration of their affected system studies," MISO added. "Thus, the seams partners propose this JPA to memorialize its procedures, consistent with [FERC] Order 2023." ENTITIES COLLABORATED OVER SUMMER The two entities collaborated from June to August to develop the JPA proposal. AECI updated its interconnection procedures and agreements in early June to incorporate many of the reforms from FERC's landmark Order 2023, including a transition to a cluster study process, and opened its first cluster application window in September. MISO said in the filing that the proposed affected system coordination procedures would largely mirror FERC-approved coordination procedures it has in place with neighboring grid operators Southwest Power Pool and the PJM Interconnection. Slight deviations were made to accommodate specific coordination needs between MISO, a FERC-regulated regional transmission organization, and AECI. The JPA will set the initial framework for transmission and interconnection planning, according to the filing. "For the purposes of transmission planning, the seams partners propose to coordinate transmission planning activities, including expansion plans, system impact studies, and other transmission planning activities," MISO said. "The seams partners intend to expand upon these general principles in the future." MISO said in the filing that some of its stakeholders had expressed some concerns about AECI's selection criteria and coordination of new load additions ahead of the submission to FERC. However, the two entities determined that these issues were outside the scope of the initial filing. — Ronnie Turner, ronnie.turner@spglobal.com-- Edited by Valarie Jackson, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

Nomadar Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nomadar Corp., the innovation arm of Cádiz CF, a professional soccer club competing in the Segunda División, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, highlighting its efforts in expanding its High Performance Training (HPT) program, hosting events, and preparing for future growth initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $877,669, primarily attributed to HPT License Agreements and event income generated from an event hosted at the Mirandilla Stadium.
  • Gross Profit: $533,981, reflecting the commencement of revenue-generating services related to the HPT program and events held at Mirandilla Stadium.
  • Net Loss: $1,410,286, driven by professional fees related to Form S-1 filing requirements and legal, accounting, and auditing services performed in preparation for the company's Direct Listing.
  • Net Loss for the Three Months Ended September 30, 2025: $496,209, primarily due to professional fees and other expenses, offset by a gain on foreign currency transactions.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Nomadar Corp is the innovation arm of Cádiz CF, a professional soccer club competing in the Segunda División. The company is developing four business verticals and is majority-owned by Sport City Cádiz, S.L.
  • Framework Agreement with Cádiz CF: On January 10, 2025, Nomadar entered into a Framework Agreement with Cádiz CF to provide technical training staff for players and integrate training methodologies into Cádiz CF’s sessions. This agreement is effective for three years and renewable by written agreement.
  • High Performance Training Program (HPT): The Nomadar HPT is designed for young athletes to study, live, and train in an elite soccer program. The program has expanded to include new clients in 2025, with training facilities based in Cádiz, Spain.
  • Stadium Events: Nomadar has a temporary, non-exclusive right to use the Mirandilla Stadium for hosting events. The company is engaging third-party event coordinators to host events, with revenue generated from event income.
  • Mágico González Brand: Nomadar plans to launch the Mágico González brand in the U.S. in the fourth quarter of 2025, with e-commerce offerings beginning at that time.
  • Multi-Purpose Event Center: Nomadar intends to construct Sportech City in Puerto de Santa Maria, Spain, featuring a venue for concerts and sporting events, a hotel, a sports clinic, and commercial spaces. Construction is scheduled to begin in 2026 and complete by 2030.
  • Future Outlook: Nomadar plans to expand the reach of the Nomadar HPT globally and leverage its association with Cádiz CF to build a loyal community of athletes and families. The company is also focused on developing Sportech City and launching the Mágico González brand in the U.S.

SEC Filing: Nomadar Corp. [ NOMA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 21, 2025
